title: Reading a Directory in UNIX System Programming
description: A flowchart illustrating the sequence of system calls and functions used to read the contents of a directory in a UNIX/Linux environment.
relationships: opendir() initializes the process and returns a DIR pointer
readdir() is called in a loop using the DIR pointer to get each directory entry
Each call to readdir() returns a struct dirent containing member d_name
After the loop, closedir() is called to free resources
Error checks should be performed after opendir() and readdir()
